Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1341649555|1341649555]] by [[Special:Contributions/Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) The character deleted in this edit is used to transliterate the Arabic letter ʿain: it is a more specific transliteration than a simple straight apostrophe, since the latter can have other meanings, such as a hamza. So, far from being incorrect, this character would be widely considered as preferable.

/* Assassination */ is a detail of the state response to the event - the actual event is: "someone was killed=is dead" "how it happened" "who did the act" these are the details of the subject "assassination" - the time of the subject is: from "the initiation of a hostile force - the moment of death" is the complete possible detail range

/* Assassination */ is information on reportage/transmission of the event isn't a detail of the actual subject of the event=what actually happened - that others know of the event not "the actual event"

/* Assassination */ is not a detail of the event - only states that the Foreign Minister was not informed which made no change/difference to the fact of the end of the individual=is irrelevant to the sequence of events of the subject
